questions.question: C++ is a compiled language.
questions.explanation: Correct! C++ code must be compiled into machine code before it can run.

questions.question: A 'Reference' must be initialized when it is created.
questions.explanation: Unlike pointers, references cannot be null and must refer to an existing object from the start.

questions.question: In C++, 'struct' members are private by default.
questions.explanation: In C++, 'struct' members are public by default, while 'class' members are private by default.

questions.question: Pointers can be re-assigned to point to different memory locations.
questions.explanation: This is a key flexibility of pointers compared to references.

questions.question: C++ supports multiple inheritance (a class inheriting from more than one base class).
questions.explanation: Yes, C++ allows a class to inherit features from multiple parents.

questions.question: The 'new' operator is used to allocate memory on the heap.
questions.explanation: Memory allocated with 'new' should eventually be freed using 'delete'.
